name: Gloria Heeter's Best Gingerbread Cookies
recipeIngredient: Currants, raisins, and candy for decoration
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 25 minutes Chilling time: 8 hours or overnight
Baking time: 7 to 10 minutes
1. Beat margarine, sugar, and molasses in a large mixing bowl until smooth. Add egg and mix well.
2. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, cloves, ginger, and nutmeg. Gradually add to butter mixture; mix until combined.
3. Divide dough into 4 equal portions on a large piece of plastic wrap. Wrap each portion and shape into flat disks about 1 inch thick. Refrigerate until firm, about 8 hours, or freeze for 2 hours. (Dough can be refrigerated up to 3 days.)
4. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Working with one disk of dough at a time, roll out on a well-floured surface, dusting the rolling pin as you work. Dough will be very soft and can be difficult to work with, so work quickly and use plenty of flour. Using cookie cutters dipped in flour, cut into desired shapes. Place cookies 1 inch apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
5. Bake until lightly puffed, about 7 to 10 minutes. The first batch may puff up more because it will have less flour rolled into the dough. Let cool slightly on wire racks before decorating with icing.
Icing: Mix confectioners' sugar with a small amount of water until thick and spreading consistency. Add food coloring if desired and place in a small plastic bag. Cut a small hole in one corner of the bag, then drizzle icing onto cooled cookies.
Note: A 4-inch gingerbread cookie cutter was used in testing. Oak Brook's Gloria Heeter is a newcomer to the Chicago area who brings her favorite gingerbread recipe from Kansas City, Kan. She replaced some egg yolks with corn oil margarine instead of shortening and sometimes adds currants or candy for decoration. For Christmas, she prints names on the cookies, decorates them with red and green ribbon, and gifts them as a heartfelt gesture.
